_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, composed of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice and indexed to 100 in 2015, stands at 141, indicating an approximate 41% increase since that baseline year. This rise has been uneven across components and largely recent rather than a sustained trend. The index aggregates diverse soft commodities, reflecting their global price movements since 2015. This snapshot highlights how certain staples consumed at breakfast have become significantly costlier over less than a decade.
notes.movers: Coffee has been the primary driver with a 99% increase since 2015, nearly doubling in price. Orange juice prices are up 34%, followed by wheat at 19% and sugar at 13%. These changes stem primarily from supply disruptions caused by weather and crop diseases—such as citrus greening and hurricanes impacting orange juice, and frost and drought affecting arabica coffee regions—rather than general inflation. Soft commodity markets like these are relatively thin, meaning that weather events and crop health can sharply influence global prices.
notes.why: The headline increase in the breakfast basket price is largely attributable to a few weather-sensitive crops rather than broad market inflation. Because these commodities depend heavily on variable environmental conditions, their elevated prices may persist for years, reflecting ongoing challenges in production rather than transient factors.
